description: Marriage contract. Groom: Munā b. Shelomo. Bride: Mubāraka bt. Avraham. Location: Fustat. Dated: 13 Tishrei 1406 Seleucid = 26 September 1094 CE. The couple is getting remarried after an earlier divorce. Although a divorcee, the bride is still a virgin. The value of the trousseau is 2 dinars and the marriage gift is 5 + 15 dinars. The witnesses include the ḥaver Yeḥezqel b. ʿEli ha-Kohen he-Ḥaver. Verso is not digitized but is reportedly blank. This is the ketubba edited by Adalbert Merx in his 'Documents de paléographie hébraïque et arabe', 1894, pp. 36–37. (Information in part from Goitein notes and index card linked below, Goitein, Med Soc III, p. 391, and MY.)
description_he: כתובת נישואין חוזרים; פוסטאט; 13 תשרי 1406 לספירת השטרות (26 בספטמבר 1094 לספירה); חתן: מונא בן שלמה; כלה: מובארקה בת אברהם, גרושה ובתולה; מוהר 20 דינרים
